key_insight: An unsupervised domain adaptation framework with gradual source domain expansion enables reliable weld penetration status prediction across fundamentally different welding processes (TIG and laser) without requiring extensive relabeling.
engineering_relevance: The approach improves intelligent welding monitoring and makes models more transferable between process types, reducing deployment cost when moving to new welding systems.
method: Train a UDA model to align feature distributions between labeled source and unlabeled target welding domains.
Use gradual source domain expansion (GSDE) to progressively incorporate source samples and stabilize cross-domain learning.
Validate with UMAP to confirm domain-invariant features and preserved class separation.
evidence_signal: The method achieved 90.65% on TIGFH and 90.72% on LSPS in same-process tests, and 80.48% TIG-to-laser plus 81.13% laser-to-TIG in cross-process transfer, far above the supervised baseline.
watch_item: Performance may still depend on how representative the source and target welding datasets are, so adaptation quality could drop when process settings or sensor conditions change substantially.
paper_title: A cross-process welding penetration status prediction algorithm based on unsupervised domain adaptation in laser and TIG welding
